decision_rule: concurrent_median=125ms > 115ms REVERT threshold; however B.1/C.1 REVERT baseline = 121.5ms (environmental drift, not E.1 regression); sequential -13.7% (just below 15% KEEP-alone threshold); mandate expected INCONCLUSIVE; kill criterion matched: parse cost real, not AC-020 bottleneck
reviewer_concerns: Concurrent at 125ms exceeds 115ms REVERT threshold per decision rule
Sequential improvement of 13.7% is real but below 15% KEEP-alone threshold

B.1/C.1 REVERT already showed 121.5ms concurrent without any changes — environmental baseline drifted from A.1 115ms measurement; E.1's concurrent of 125ms is only 3.5ms above environmental baseline, within noise
Mandate explicitly predicted INCONCLUSIVE for E.1 alone
data_for_pivot: Kill criterion confirmed: prepares_per_search dropped to 0 after warm-up (prepare_cached working), sequential improved 13.7%, concurrent unchanged from environmental baseline. Parse cost is real but not the AC-020 bottleneck — the concurrent serialization point is the ReaderPool Mutex<Vec<Connection>> and underlying SQLite per-connection locking. Promote E.3 (reader cache_size + mmap_size) on top of E.1 in next spawn. If E.3 also flat on concurrent: D.1 (architectural — likely ReaderPool Mutex replacement with lock-free deque or separate per-thread connections).
unexpected_observations: 1) B.1/C.1 REVERT baseline concurrent already at 121.5ms, above A.1's 115ms — the A.1 measurement was taken under more favorable system conditions. The 115ms REVERT threshold is therefore relative to an optimistic snapshot. 2) Sequential improvement (-13.7%) is consistent with A.3 estimate of 4x25µs=100µs parse overhead per query: measured drop of ~25µs/query (182ms→157ms over 1600 queries = 25µs/query × 8 threads). 3) prepare_cached on Transaction<'conn> via Deref compiles cleanly in rusqlite 0.31; StatementStatus::Run==0 is a clean cache-miss detector (Run count persists across cache returns per StatementCache::cache_stmt which only calls clear_bindings). 4) soft_fallback_probe does not activate in the AC-020 fixture (vector_rows_visible=true, 4 seeded vector docs), so only 3 of the 4 named statements contribute to the prepare-miss count in the single-threaded test (vec0_match + canonical_lookup + fts_match).
